channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

item.title: We Need a Bigger Boat! Where To Watch Shark Week 2026
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Watch Shark Week 2026 on Discovery Channel, Max, YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV and more. Stream all 20 new premieres July 26–Aug. 1.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25+ years covering horror and streaming.Shark Week 2026 is back for its 38th annual celebration of all things sharks. This year, Discovery Channel is premiering 20 all-new specials starting Sunday, July 26 at 8 p.m. ET/PT and running through Saturday, Aug. 1. The lineup includes fan-favorite franchises like “Air Jaws” plus bold new stories, prehistoric shark reconstructions using cutting-edge CGI, and unexpected collaborations that blend science with spectacle. Whether you are a longtime Shark Week devotee or a first-time viewer, you have got plenty of ways to tune in — from traditional cable to streaming services to free trials.

Looking for the hottest entertainment?

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Where to watch Shark Week 2026 on streaming and cable TV

Streaming service Cable channel
Max (formerly HBO Max) Discovery Channel
View Plans See Channel Number in Your Area
Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

When does Shark Week 2026 stream on HBO Max?

New episodes premiere live on Discovery Channel and stream the same night on HBO Max. Subscribers with access to live Discovery Channel can watch each nightly premiere at 8 p.m. ET/PT. If your HBO Max plan does not include live channels, episodes are typically available on demand the next day.

When does Shark Week 2026 air on Discovery Channel?

All 20 new specials air live on Discovery Channel nightly from July 26 through Aug. 1, with each episode premiering at 8 p.m. ET/PT. If you miss a live broadcast, you can catch reruns throughout the week. Cable subscribers can also stream episodes through the Discovery Go app with a valid cable login.

What channel is Discovery Channel?

City DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Spectrum channel Xfinity channel
New York, NY 278 182 27 66 —
Los Angeles, CA 278 182 — 73 —
Chicago, IL 278 182 — — 51
Houston, TX 278 182 — 26 29
Phoenix, AZ 278 182 — — 43
Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change. Don’t see your hometown or provider? We have got channel number guides for every popular local and cable TV channel, including Discovery Channel, in over 50 U.S. cities.

Which live TV providers have Shark Week 2026

Provider Price Live cable channel Streaming service Details
Cox $20.00–$184.00/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Cox

Read Cox review
Best for sports
DIRECTV 		$89.99–$169.99/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for DIRECTV STREAM

Read DIRECTV review
DISH $89.99–$119.99/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for DISH

Read DISH review
Hulu + Live TV $88.99–$99.99/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Hulu

Read Hulu + Live TV review
Optimum $15.00–$140.00/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Optimum

Read Optimum review
Philo $33.00/mo. ✔ — View Plan |

Read Philo review
Sling TV $19.99–$60.99/mo. ✔ — View Plans for Sling TV

Read Sling TV review
Editor"s Choice
Spectrum 		$40.00–$145.00/mo. ✔ ✔ Check Availability

Read Spectrum review
Best of the Best
Verizon Fios 		$105.00–$149.00/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Verizon

Read Verizon Fios review
Xfinity $65.00–$135.00/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Xfinity

Read Xfinity review
Best for live TV streaming
YouTube TV 		$82.99/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for YouTube TV

Read YouTube TV review
Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change. Shark Week remains one of the most accessible summer programming events, with Discovery Channel available on nearly every major cable, satellite, and live TV streaming bundle. Whether you are looking for the ultimate shark-themed experience or just want to catch the premiere, you have got options that fit your viewing style.

Find the best TV provider near you

Want a TV plan that keeps you covered beyond Shark Week? Enter your zip code below to see your area’s top-ranked TV and internet bundles.

Where to watch Shark Week 2026 FAQ

CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: Fubo is Raising Prices By $15—Here’s How It Justifies the Increase
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Fubo just signed a new deal with NBC, and that means your bill is going up. Read on to learn why Fubo is raising prices.
item.content:encoded:
Good news and bad news for Fubo customers

I put the bad news in the headline, so let’s rip the bandage off. The live TV streaming service Fubo is getting more expensive to the tune of $15/mo. this month. Your total bill will vary depending on your plan, and whether you subscribe to regional sports networks, but either way you’re about to pay more money than you’d probably like. The good news is that you’re getting more content for the price. Fubo is bringing NBC channels back to its lineup, including all the sports that NBC hosts. That means you’ll get Sunday Night Football, Sunday Night Baseball, WNBA games, and more, all bundled into your Fubo subscription. Fubo had previously removed NBC channels from its lineup after disputes with NBCUniversal, but that dispute finally ended in June. Fubo is still missing Warner Bros. Discovery channels like AMC, BBC America, and the Food Network, as well as Versant channels like SYFY, USA Network, and MS NOW. Instead, Fubo mainly focuses on sports content and little else. Fubo’s got a lot of plans, and sports add-ons tend to get pretty confusing under the best of circumstances, so let’s dive into the details and figure out what this means for you and your TV.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Which Fubo plans are changing?

Plan Old price New price
Fubo Sports (Sports + News) $55.99/mo. $64.99/mo.
Essential $73.99/mo. $88.99/mo.
Pro $73.99/mo. $88.99/mo.
Elite $83.99/mo. $98.99/mo.
Ultimate $98.99/mo. $113.99/mo.
Deluxe $103.99/mo. $118.99/mo.
All of Fubo’s regular plans (Essential, Pro, Elite, Ultimate, and Deluxe) are going up by $15 per month. Its Core and Ultra plans, only available in select markets, are also going up by $15 per month. If you subscribe to the Fubo Sports service, also known as Sports + News, your plan will be going up by $9 per month. In addition, according to Fubo’s website, if you subscribe to a plan with a Regional Sports Fee and live in Boston, Philadelphia, or an area serviced by NBC Sports Bay Area or NBC Sports California, your fee will rise to $15.99/mo. All of these changes will go into effect when NBCUniversal channels become available in your region. Fubo first announced these changes back in June, but some are only going into effect now in July, which is why it may be on your radar. Unfortunately, there is no one single price-changing day to report: it will vary from person to person, so you should plan to see the change on your next bill. You can’t opt out of receiving NBCUniversal content on your existing plan, but you can downgrade your subscription if you’d like to keep your monthly bill low. You can also consider switching to another provider—we’ve got a lot of recommendations if you’re looking for a new live TV streaming service.

What does this mean for Latino subscribers?

There’s also good news and bad news for Latino subscribers. The good news is that Fubo Latino gained Telemundo and Universo back in June, so you’re already getting FIFA World Cup courage and lots of other Spanish-language sports. In fact, you got FIFA coverage before Fubo’s English-language customers. The bad news is that your plan also got more expensive for your first billing date on or after July 1. From now on, you’ll be paying $19.99/mo., rather than the $14.99/mo. it cost previously.

What channels is Fubo gaining with the price increase?

Thanks to this new deal, Fubo is gaining a bunch of NBCUniversal sports coverage. Unfortunately, this deal doesn’t include Versant channels, so Fubo is still missing favorites like SYFY, USA Network, and MS NOW. Your new lineup will change based on your plan and location, but here’s the full list of possible channels and networks:

  • NBC
  • Telemundo*
  • Bravo
  • Universo*
  • NBC Sports Regional Sports Channels
  • NBCSN
  • Cozi
  • NBC News NOW
  • True CRMZ
*also included with Fubo Latino
Of course, if it’s only NBC you’re after, you could always pick it up with an over-the-air (OTA) antenna. And if you’re looking for both live NBC coverage and Bravo content, Peacock Premium Plus has all you could want and more. But if you want a sports-focused streaming service and don’t mind shelling out the extra money, it is nice that Fubo has all of this extra coverage now. NBC hosts a lot of exclusive sports programming, like Sunday Night Football, Sunday Night Baseball, English Premier League, WNBA games, and more.

Fubo

Fubo has lost a lot of popular channels in recent years, but now it’s getting NBCUniversal programming back, adding to its already robust sports lineup.

YouTube TV

YouTube TV is our Editor’s Choice when it comes to sports-centric streaming services. Its robust channel lineup and reliable performance really wowed our Sports Editor.

Peacock

Peacock is a great alternative if you’re only looking for NBCUniversal content. It’s got titles from NBC, Bravo, and more.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • 12 States Try to Block Paramount Deal
  • World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs
  • How Much Does a Movie Ticket Cost in 2026?

item.title: Bundesliga Moves to USA Network and Fandango in New U.S. Rights Deal
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Germany’s top soccer league reached a broadcast agreement with USA Network and Fandango. Here’s what the deal means for Bundesliga fans in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
USA Network and Fandango will carry all Bundesliga matches

Germany’s top-flight soccer league has a new broadcast home in the U.S. USA Sports acquired exclusive English-language rights to air Bundesliga games through the 2030-2031 season, the company announced. The league’s 300-plus live matches will air on USA Network and stream on Fandango (formerly Fandango at Home) starting in August. USA Network will carry at least 30 matches featuring the top Bundesliga teams and rivalries. Every other match will stream for free on Fandango. “The Bundesliga boasts some of the most well-known clubs and players in the world and has an established connection with American soccer fans,” Matt Hong, president of USA Sports, said in a statement. “Our partnership with the Bundesliga represents another significant milestone in the growth of USA Sports as we continue to build a premier destination for live sports and deliver more of the events fans care about most.”

What the new Bundesliga TV deal means for U.S. fans

Before this deal, fans mainly saw Bundesliga games through ESPN+, with select games airing on ESPN, ESPN2, and even ABC. For the few games on ABC, at least, German football lovers could use a digital antenna to watch them for free. But there’s really no way to access USA Network for free — not for the long term, anyway. Now, fans will absolutely need a paid TV plan for more than two dozen games. The good news: Fandango will stream a ton of games for free. For Spanish-language fans — or people who just prefer watching football in Spanish — Telemundo signed a three-year deal with the Bundesliga to broadcast games. Telemundo and Universo will air more than 100 games over the air and on cable, and every game will stream live on Peacock. Fans of Premier League soccer are already accustomed to watching games on USA Network and Telemundo. But Bundesliga fans new to the setup will need a TV plan to watch matches on USA Network, Telemundo, and Universo once the season starts.

Bundesliga looks to build on World Cup viewing momentum

Record numbers of viewers have been tuning in to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Historically, the global tournament has attracted new fans from every age demographic. It’s likely that trend will continue, and the Bundesliga is clearly banking on that with these new partnerships. “The 2026 World Cup has highlighted the tremendous growth potential of soccer in the U.S.,” said Robin Austermann, Bundesliga Americas Executive Vice President, in a statement. “We’ve seen that momentum firsthand, with the number of Bundesliga fans in the U.S. growing by 43% over the past five years. “Our partnership with Versant’s USA Sports marks the next phase of our strategy, combining outstanding distribution with significant growth opportunities by bringing the Bundesliga closer to more fans across the United States than ever before.”

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

CableTV.com verdict: A big win for U.S. fans, even if they have to pay more

If you’re thinking, “Where do I get USA Network?” after this deal, you’re likely not alone. USA Network hasn’t been part of NBCUniversal since January. That’s when Versant became an independent company after Comcast spun it off, taking USA Network, Fandango, Golf Channel, and many other NBCU brands with it. That corporate shuffle may make the Bundesliga’s new English-language setup a little harder to follow for fans who still associate USA Network with NBCUniversal. Fortunately, fans can look to DIRECTV, YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, or Sling TV to get USA Network. But, like I mentioned earlier, the days of occasional free marquee Bundesliga games on ABC are over. Be that as it may, more access to a league or sport fans love is never a bad thing. The above TV providers each have some sort of combination of USA Network, Telemundo, and Universo. And with the addition of Fandango, fans can still enjoy plenty of free soccer. In other words, if you’re in the U.S. and love German soccer (or are more curious after the World Cup), you have so many ways to watch live once the season kicks off on Friday, August 28. That just wasn’t the case before.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs: Will Casual Soccer Viewing Continue?
  • Jalen Brunson, Messi, Shohei Ohtani Highlight 2026 ESPYs Nominations
  • Premier League Fixture Release Brings Key Changes

item.title: Catty, Cringey, Lusty! 8 New Reality Series Streaming in July
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Our reality TV expert breaks down 7 new reality series coming to streaming in April 2026. Check out their curated picks.
item.content:encoded:
With “Love Island,” “Summer House,” and “Temptation Island” in the rearview for the beginning of the summer, there are still so many reality shows we can indulge in. Things are heating up in this summer forecast of mess. Here’s my suggestion: maybe instead of the sun burning our skin, why don’t we just stay home and watch reality TV unfold in front of us? Come on, there’s cool air and ice aplenty in there. To convince you further, there are plenty of shows returning to our TV screens, including The OGs of the OC – “The Real Housewives of Orange County”, the Korean makeover marvel “Better Late Than Single”, and a new show featuring Bravo’s Vanderpump Rules alum– “House of Stassi.” Put that sunscreen back in the drawer and bring on the mess! Here’s everything you need to know when it comes to July’s reality TV watchlist!

8. “Better Late Than Single” (July 7 – Netflix)

This Korean reality makeover show helps “eternal singles” find love by coaching them on communication, style, and more. They’re then put together in a house to see whether love blossoms with the other “eternal singles” or whether it leads to heartbreak. This sleeper hit will make you kick your feet, giggle, or yell at the television because you just want them to work!

Where to watch “Better Late Than Single”

You can catch the first and second season of “Better Late Than Single” out now on Netflix with a subscription.

7. “Big Brother 28” (July 9 – CBS, Paramount+ Premium)

”Big Brother” is back, and if you’re looking to dial into season 28, now is the time! “Big Brother” features houseguests who are cut off from the outside world and filmed 24/7. Within the house, they compete in challenges and form relationships, friendships, enemies, and alliances among the houseguests. If you’re looking for an Orwellian-esque MTV’s “The Real World,” jump into “Big Brother” as soon as possible!

Where to watch “Big Brother 28”

”Big Brother” airs Wednesday, Thursday, and Sunday at 8 p.m. ET on CBS, which you can watch on YouTube TV or Paramount+ Premium.

6. “The Real Housewives of Orange County” (July 8 – Bravo, Peacock)

”The Real Housewives of Orange County” is in its 20th season and has become the longest-running in the franchise. We see some old faces returning, including Vicki Gunvalson (aka the OG of the OC), and welcome newcomer Camella Garcia, former Playboy model and wrestler turned real estate agent.

Where to watch “The Real Housewives of Orange County”

You can catch “The Real Housewives of Orange County” weekly on Thursdays at 8 p.m. ET on Bravo, which you can get with YouTube TV. Or watch the next day on Peacock with a subscription.

5. “Project Runway” (July 9 – Freeform, Hulu, and Disney+)

Fresh off the catwalk, season 22 is already hitting the ground strutting. (Did you like that wordplay? Don’t roll your eyes!) Heidi Klum returns with Christian Siriano and a panel of A-list judges to launch one designer into superstardom. What’s at stake? Oh, just a cash prize of $200,000, a mentorship to help debut their brand, and a spread in “Elle” magazine. No big deal, right?

Where to watch “Project Runway”

Catch “Project Runway” on Thursdays at 10 p.m., but catch episodes on Hulu and Disney+ the next day.

4. “Married at First Sight” (July 12 – Peacock)

Have you ever thought of marrying someone sight unseen? No? Well, I have a show for you! “Married at First Sight” brings singles together with the help of relationship experts and walks them down the aisle. When the couples say “I do,” that’s where the real tests begin. Will the experts actually know what they’re doing in matching these couples? Will they stay in these marriages, or will they fall apart?

Where to watch “Married at First Sight”

Watch “Married at First Sight” every Thursday on Peacock with a subscription.

3. “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter” (July 12 – Netflix)

Of the “Love Is Blind” spinoffs, “Love Is Blind: UK” is top-tier entertainment. “Love Is Blind: UK” brings singles to find their match sight unseen. When they meet, they test their connection by mixing their worlds, including living together. Will they be ready to walk down the aisle and say, “I do”? “Love is Blind: UK – After the Altar” brings back our favorites (and not-so-favorites) as they talk about how life has been since their pod experiences and confront each other on old grudges.

Where to watch “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter”

Watch the three-episode “Love is Blind: UK – After the Alter” now on Netflix with a subscription.

3. “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” (July 15 – Netflix)

Known for hosting “Love Is Blind” and “Perfect Match,” Nick and Vanessa Lachey have graced us once again with one of Netflix’s most diabolical creations. “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” features couples who are at a crossroads. One wants to be married, and the other one is on the fence. They test their couple by switching partners with other couples to see if they are the right fit or if there’s someone else out there who may be a better match.

Where to watch “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On”

Catch “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” on July 14, and the finale and reunion on July 22, on Netflix with a subscription.

1. “House of Stassi” (July 29 – Freeform, Hulu)

Love her or hate her, Stassi Schroeder will always be a mainstay of reality TV. Debuting on “Vanderpump Rules,” Stassi has always given us quick quips, running lines, and mountains of controversy. With “House of Stassi,” the audience will get to see why Stassi has continued to be a talked-about figure. Stassi is joined by her husband, Beau Clark, best friend Taylor Strecker, alums from “Vanderpump Rules,” Katie Maloney and Kristina Kelly, and more.

Where to watch “House of Stassi”

Tune into the season premiere of “House of Stassi” Wednesday, July 29 on Freeform, which is available with YouTube TV. Or watch it the next day on Hulu with a subscription.

Disney+

You can watch “Project Runway” with a Disney+ subscription ($11.99–$18.99/mo.).

Hulu

Catch “Project Runway” and “House of Stassi” with a Hulu subscription ($11.99–$18.99/mo.).

Netflix

Best for KidsYou’ll need a Netflix subscription ($8.99–$26.99/mo.) ($8.99–$26.99/mo.) to watch “Better Late Than Single,” “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter,” and “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On.”

Paramount+

You can get your live CBS channel, and watch “Big Brother Season 28,” with a subscription to the Paramount+ Premium plan ($8.99–$13.99/mo.).

Peacock

A Peacock subscription ($7.99–$16.99/mo.) is one way to watch “The Real Housewives of Orange County” and “Married at First Sight.”

YouTube TV

Best overallYou can find live TV channels like Bravo, CBS and Freeform on this top-shelf live TV streaming service (($82.99/mo.).

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

What to binge next

  • What To Watch on Netflix
  • New Shows Coming to Netflix
  • What To Watch on Peacock

item.title: Where To Watch ‘Dexter’ on Streaming and Cable
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Find out where to watch Dexter, including Paramount+ with SHOWTIME streaming and cable channel numbers by city.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Mike Strayer, our Managing Editor with 11 years covering streaming and entertainment.
Status: Data verified on (7/15/26)
Dexter Morgan’s double life as a Miami blood-spatter analyst by day and vigilante serial killer by night made him one of TV’s most iconic antiheroes, and his storyline has recently been resurrected from the IP catecombs. Between its original eight seasons, “Dexter: New Blood,” “Dexter: Original Sin,” and “Dexter: Resurrection,” there’s a lot of Dark Passenger to track down. Good news: The entire Dexter universe is all available in one place.

Where to watch “Dexter” on streaming and cable TV

Swipe Left to See All →
Streaming serviceCable channel
Paramount+ with SHOWTIMEParamount+ with SHOWTIME
View Plans for Paramount PlusSee Channel Number in Your Area

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

When does “Dexter” stream on Paramount+ with SHOWTIME?

Every episode of the original “Dexter” series is currently streaming on Paramount+, and it’s included in every Paramount+ plan tier. The newer spinoffs, “Dexter: Original Sin” and “Dexter: Resurrection,” require the Paramount+ Premium plan and air new seasons weekly.

When does “Dexter” air live on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME channel?

Reruns of “Dexter” still air periodically on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME linear channel (formerly SHOWTIME), so cable and satellite subscribers with that premium tier can catch episodes without opening an app. If you’re looking for premium TV recs, check out our full guide to what to watch on Paramount+ with SHOWTIME.

What channel is Paramount+ with SHOWTIME?

City DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Xfinity channel
New York, NY 545/546 318/319 320/327 —
Los Angeles, CA 545/546 318/319 — —
Chicago, IL 545/546 318/319 — 185
Houston, TX 545/546 318/319 — 474
Phoenix, AZ 545/546 318/319 — —

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

Don’t see your hometown or provider? We’ve got channel number guides for every popular local and cable TV channel, including Paramount+ with SHOWTIME, in over 50 U.S. cities.

Which live TV providers have “Dexter”?

Provider Price Live cable channel Streaming service Details

Best for Sports

Read DIRECTV review
DISH $89.99–$119.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for DISH

Read DISH review
Fubo $14.99–$103.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for FuboTV

Read Fubo review
Hulu + Live TV $88.99–$99.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Hulu

Read Hulu + Live TV review
Optimum $15.00–$140.00/mo. ✔ (Add-on) ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Optimum

Read Optimum review
Sling TV $19.99–$60.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Sling TV

Read Sling TV review
Editor"s Choice

Read YouTube TV review

Our editorial team weighs pricing, channel lineup, and customer satisfaction data to rank providers for our Best of the Best Awards. We recommend a DIRECTV streaming plan if you want live Paramount+ with SHOWTIME, want access to the Paramount+ app, and love live sports. If you’d rather stream on demand than deal with cable, Paramount+ with SHOWTIME also carries solid channel hubs, like CBS and BET, on top of every OG “Dexter” season.

Find the best TV provider near you

Want a TV plan that keeps you covered beyond episodes of “Dexter”? Enter your zip code below to see your area’s top-ranked TV and internet bundles.

Where to watch “Dexter” FAQ

CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: DIRECTV Slashes Sports Prices Just in Time for the World Cup Final
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Catch the World Cup final and save on sports. Get DIRECTV’s MySports Pack for $49.99/mo. and a free month of MySports Extra for a limited time.
item.content:encoded:
DIRECTV’s offering $20 sports discounts for a limited time

Plenty of providers are using the World Cup to sling deals to new subscribers. But for year-round sports fans, it doesn’t get much better than DIRECTV, our perennial pick for the Best Streaming Service for Sports (and the most popular streamer for soccer fans according to this year’s Customer Satisfaction Survey, too). Here are the details: DIRECTV is currently discounting its MySports® Genre Pack® to just $49.99/mo. for the first two months. With MySports, you’ll get access to 20+ channels, including the necessary FOX and FS1 to catch the rest of the matches. That includes the World Cup final, which will be played on July 19 at 3:00 p.m. ET on FOX. On top of the $20 off deal, you’ll also get a month of free MySports Extra® for a limited time. This Mini Pack (which can only be purchased as an add-on to the larger MySports Genre Pack) adds an additional 8+ channels, including NFL Redzone and MLB Strikezone. Just keep in mind that after one month is up, MySports Extra will renew at its going rate of $13.99/mo. plus tax. And after the second month, MySports will return to its regular price of $69.00/mo., plus tax.

Hook it up with the hottest in TV and internet

Stay tuned for the latest deals, show premieres, and more by signing up for our newsletter.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Spanish speakers will need to upgrade, though

Unfortunately, if you’re a Spanish-speaking fútbol lover who wants to take advantage of this slick deal, you’ll need to look elsewhere. The DIRECTV MySports and MySports Extra Packs don’t include the two channels you need to watch the World Cup in Spanish—Telemundo and UNIVERSO. Lucky for you, I compiled an exhaustive list of the best 2026 FIFA World Cup deals just last month. You’ll be able to take your pick from multiple discounts that’ll let you catch the World Cup finals in Spanish, and save some dinero while you’re at it.Loyal to DIRECTV?: Even though the MySports deal won’t be much use to you if you want to watch the World Cup finals en español, DIRECTV offers plenty of other ways to watch the Cup in multiple languages. Check out our How To Watch the World Cup on DIRECTV guide for all the details.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for sports fans

  • More Soccer Fans Choose DIRECTV Than Any Other Streamer
  • World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs
  • Jalen Brunson, Messi, Shohei Ohtani Highlight 2026 ESPYs Nominations

item.title: California Leads 12-State Effort to Block Paramount/Warner Bros. Deal
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: 12 states, led by California, are suing Paramount Skydance in an effort to block the media giant’s Warner Bros. Discovery acquisition.
item.content:encoded:
The case will likely delay Paramount’s plans

I’ve been following the Paramount situation for some time now; Paramount Skydance announced its intention to buy Warner Bros. Discovery out from under Netflix back in March, and so far no one’s been able to stop it. Normally, the U.S. government might try to enact anti-trust protections (you know, the ones you learned about in 8th grade Social Studies), but it’s been extremely lax on the subject in recent years. Enter the Attorneys General of 12 U.S. states. Led by California’s Attorney General Rob Bonta, the group is suing to block the acquisition. Bonta argues that the deal would “create a media behemoth” that would negatively impact the economy. The case is also being presented in defense of local movie theaters, who would lose out on revenue if coerced by a film distribution monopoly. And cable TV providers — the bread and butter of our own website — would lose negotiation leverage, causing increased prices for consumers. In Bonta’s words, “Your cable bill is going to go up.” The states’ case is aiming to reduce costs for the everyday person — or at least stop them from rising even further — by stopping Paramount’s Warner Bros. acquisition. It’s too early to say how the case will end, but keep reading for the details we know about right now.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Which states are participating?

Attorneys General from 12 states are banding together in an effort to stop Paramount. Here’s the full list in alphabetical order:

  • Arizona
  • California
  • Colorado
  • Connecticut
  • Massachusetts
  • Minnesota
  • Nevada
  • New Jersey
  • New Mexico
  • New York
  • Oregon
  • Washington

What does this mean for your subscriptions?

Paramount Skydance hasn’t been quiet about its plans to combine HBO Max and Paramount+ into one giant streamer to rival Netflix. If David Ellison’s plans go through, you’ll have fewer subscriptions and a higher bill to pay. There will be less competition forcing prices down, making things worse for both consumers and workers. That’s why those 12 states are moving to block the deal. According to Reuters, this case could take months—and that kind of delay will cost Paramount a lot of money no matter how it’s resolved. Paramount even has to pay an extra $650 million in fees if it doesn’t close the deal by October. That means that these 12 states have created an actual, tangible threat to Paramount’s plans, not just a symbolic gesture.

Paramount+

Paramount+ is three streaming services in one; it also contains everything from the now-defunct SHOWTIME and BET+ streaming services. Will HBO Max be its next acquisition?

HBO Max

In addition to its HBO library, HBO Max also hosts Discovery+ content. That makes it a really appealing target for David Ellison’s growing monopoly.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • How Much Does a Movie Ticket Cost?
  • PlayStation deletes VOD Titles
  • Apple TV Gives Free Upgrades

item.title: World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs: Will Casual Soccer Viewing Continue?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has drawn record viewership. With global soccer leagues starting up again in the coming months, will those fans tune in?
item.content:encoded:
World Cup breaking viewership records in English and Spanish

When Romelu Lukaku scored the last goal for Belgium in the 4-1 rout of the United States, you could practically hear the collective groan coming from USMNT fans, and the jubilation from Belgians. The palpable nature of that moment was underscored by the sheer volume of people watching the game live on TV. This week, FOX Sports announced that more than 33 million fans watched that matchup — the “most-watched soccer telecast in U.S. history,” per the company. Viewership peaked at more than 41 million. Days earlier, Telemundo and Peacock had their own record-breaking telecast. The Mexico-England game drew 23.2 million viewers across broadcast, digital, and mobile platforms. It was “the most-watched soccer match in total audience in Spanish-language media history,” the companies said. It’s not surprising that a worldwide competition would get a massive number of fans tuning in. But these viewership numbers could have big implications for other soccer leagues around the world.

Casual fans could follow other soccer competitions after the World Cup

If history is an indicator, soccer leagues in the U.S. and Europe — and perhaps elsewhere — could benefit from the 2026 World Cup’s popularity. Studies show that interest in international soccer competitions rises soon after a World Cup. For example, Copa América averaged just over 700,000 viewers in the U.S. in 2021, but that jumped to more than 1.6 million in 2024, per Nielsen data. The previous World Cup occurred in 2022. The men’s UEFA Euro saw viewership increase from 1.2 million to 1.5 million between the same years. It’s possible that viewership is up so much this year because the U.S. was a host country in 2026, along with Mexico and Canada. It remains to be seen what impact this World Cup will have on soccer fandom at large in the near future. Nonetheless, fans getting more exposure to great players from other countries often translates into them wanting to keep up with those players later.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

World Cup stars will continue their seasons on club teams

Many of the players who have stood out during the 2026 World Cup play for elite soccer clubs around the world. Generally, those clubs don’t get as much attention during the season, but this is the perfect time for curious fans to start getting familiar with them. Erling Haaland of Norway plays for Manchester City, which competes in the Premier League. Kylian Mbappé of France is a forward for Real Madrid, a team in La Liga. Argentina’s Lionel Messi plays for MLS team Inter Miami CF. And there are many more. Below is a breakdown of upcoming soccer leagues and what services fans can use to watch more of their new obsession.

Apple TV

Major League SoccerApple TV is the only place where fans can watch MLS. Live games of every team are included with a subscription with no blackouts. The MLS season resumes a few days before the World Cup ends.

ESPN Unlimited

La LigaLa Liga games start in mid-August for the 2026-2027 season and will be broadcast on multiple ESPN channels. ESPN Unlimited carries every ESPN-owned channel on its platform.

Fubo

Ligue 1Ligue 1, the top-flight league in France, has a distribution deal with beIN SPORTS in the U.S. Fubo offers a standalone beIN SPORTS plan for just $5.99 per month.

Peacock Premium

Premier LeagueThe English Premier League starts August 21. It has a broadcast deal with NBC Sports, and about half of the matches can be streamed on Peacock Premium. The service also includes a multiview feature. But fans will need a TV plan that includes USA Network for the rest of the matches.

Paramount+

Serie AAll matches for Italy’s top domestic league will stream on Paramount+. Matches start August 22.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Peacock Unveils New Feature for Select World Cup Knockout Matches
  • Jalen Brunson, Messi, Shohei Ohtani Highlight 2026 ESPYs Nominations
  • Caitlin Clark, Trinity Rodman, and More to Highlight New ESPN Sunday Showcase

item.title: If You’re a PAW Patrol Fan, There’s Only One Streaming Service You NEED to Have
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Don’t miss a rescue! Our guide shows you where to watch PAW Patrol episodes, movies, and specials, with options for budget viewers and completionists. Find your perfect plan now.
item.content:encoded:
“PAW Patrol” is on a lot of streaming services

You’ll find “PAW Patrol” episodes on Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, and even the Nickelodeon website. But not all “PAW Patrol” archives are made equal. For example, I wouldn’t recommend watching “PAW Patrol” on The Roku Channel. It claims to carry episodes, but it really only features two-minute clips and social content that’ll wreck your kids’ attention spans.

So read on for my top choices for streaming “PAW Patrol” on services that carry full episodes and have decent protections to keep kids from accidentally watching inappropriate content. Here’s a hint: There’s one streaming service that outshines the rest, with 11 full seasons and ample parental controls.

I want to watch “PAW Patrol” for free!

”PAW Patrol” airs on Nick Jr., which is a programming block on Nickelodeon. Unfortunately, Nickelodeon access can be expensive (more on that later).

But if you don’t care about watching things in order, you don’t need a pricey streaming service or cable plan to watch “PAW Patrol” episodes. There are two places on the ‘net where you can watch the show for free.

The first is Nickelodeon’s website. Unfortunately, you won’t find a lot of choices; you’ll get a random episode featuring the character of your choice if you click on their face. This isn’t the best way to watch “PAW Patrol”, but it is free and easy.

Watch on Nick.com
If you’re looking for a larger selection of “PAW Patrol” episodes, check out YouTube. You’re probably used to YouTube’s interface, and you can click around and watch as many episodes and clips as you want. There are even livestreams that mimic a real TV channel.

But this too comes with a catch: YouTube isn’t quite as safe as a traditional streaming service like Netflix or Paramount+. You shouldn’t leave your kids unsupervised with YouTube, especially if they’re young enough to watch Nick Jr. shows. Even YouTube Kids isn’t perfect at filtering out unwanted content. So YouTube can be a great resource—if you’re there watching “PAW Patrol” with your kids. Not so much as a digital babysitter.

Looking for the hottest entertainment?

Subscribe to our email newsletter to get the latest TV premieres, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

I want to watch every “PAW Patrol” episode in order!

The best streaming service for “PAW Patrol” fans is Paramount+. It carries every episode from the first 11 seasons of “PAW Patrol,” as well as a ton of spin-off specials and movies. Netflix and Amazon Prime Video also carry “PAW Patrol” content, but only a couple of seasons each.

Paramount+ is one of our favorite streaming services for kids, and you can feel pretty safe leaving your kid with Paramount+ in its Kids’ Mode. Your child won’t be able to leave their profile without a passcode, and you can pick an age range to make all of their content is age-appropriate.

Just remember that Paramount+ doesn’t carry new PAW Patrol episodes. It takes a while before Paramount+ gets the streaming rights from the live TV providers. If you want to watch anything new, you’ll have to read on.

Service Seasons
Paramount+ 1–11
Amazon Prime Video 1–2
Netflix 2–3

I want to watch new “PAW Patrol” episodes!

To watch new “PAW Patrol” episodes, you’ll need a cable, satellite, or live TV package. This is because new “PAW Patrol” episodes air on Nick Jr. before they come to streaming—so you’ll need a plan that carries Nickelodeon.

Unfortunately, new episodes don’t stick around forever. They’ll expire after a few weeks, so you’ll want to stay on top of the “PAW Patrol” schedule.

If you’re used to streaming service interfaces already, Hulu + Live TV will probably be a good fit for you. It’s important that you pick the Live TV option—the regular on-demand Hulu package won’t have Nickelodeon.

If you’re more of an old-school household, you may want to go with a cable or satellite package. You’ll need to pick one that carries Nickelodeon. Overwhelmed by options? Don’t be! Your options will vary based on your location, and we’re here to help narrow them down further for you.

Enter your zip code below to find out more information about plans and prices.

Upgrade to a TV plan with Nickelodeon

Enter your zip code below to explore your area’s top cable and satellite providers.

Best Places to Watch “PAW Patrol”

Paramount+

Paramount+ is a fantastic streaming services for kids and families. In addition to “PAW Patrol,” it hosts a bunch of other kids’ programs and has its own robust age restriction system.

Netflix

Netflix also has a lot of content for kids, and often has enough “PAW Patrol” content to keep the kids entertained for an afternoon.

“PAW Patrol” FAQ

Is "PAW Patrol" on Netflix or Hulu?

Netflix has some "PAW Patrol" episodes, but only seasons 2 and 3. Regular Hulu doesn't have "PAW Patrol," but Hulu + Live TV does have the most recent seasons.

Where can I watch all seasons of "PAW Patrol?"

Paramount+ has the most "PAW Patrol" content, with 11 seasons at the time of writing. So if you want to watch every season in order, you should start there. To watch the latest seasons, either wait for them to come to streaming or get a cable, satellite, or live TV plan that carries Nickelodeon.

Is "PAW Patrol" on Disney+?

No, "PAW Patrol" is not on Disney+.

Who we are

If you’re trying to find shows for your kids to watch, you want to know you’re sending them to a service you can trust. That’s why, here at CableTV.com, we look into the fine print so we can recommend the best streaming services for kids—and the best shows on each platform. (We also recommend shows for adults, too. In fact, that’s most of what we do!) For more on our process, head over to How We Rank.

Related articles

  • Where to Watch Sesame Street
  • Sesame Street Partners with Netflix and YouTube
  • Does Netflix Have Movies for Kids?
  • The Best Kids’ Christmas Movies

item.title: How Much Does a Movie Ticket Cost in 2026? State-by-State Prices and Date Night Totals.
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: How much will a movie ticket cost you in 2026. We analyzed movie theater pricing data in every state and found that the average movie ticket costs $16.30.
item.content:encoded:
Streaming isn’t the only reason people aren’t going to the movies. As with many things, it comes down to money. Inflation is a thing, and fewer butts in seats means theater chains have to charge more to fill them. In 2026, the national average cost for an individual movie ticket is $16.30, while a typical movie date night averages $54.08. Staying home doesn’t sound like such a bad idea now, does it? I mean, with those prices, it makes sense why only 7% of Americans prefer watching movies in a theater. Watching a movie — or multiple movies — from your own couch is cheaper and easier. We go to the movies because they’re magical. It’s hard to beat seeing a summer blockbuster on a massive screen with buttery popcorn and a hot date. But how much is that experience worth? That’s for you to decide, but we’ve got you covered on the numbers. We analyzed chain movie theater prices state by state to determine the average cost of a movie ticket in 2026.* We also calculated the cost of that magical, mythical movie date (two tickets, a large popcorn, and two drinks). Keep reading to discover how your state ranked. * Rhode Island and Vermont were omitted from this piece due to insufficient chain theater locations.

Movie ticket pricing key findings

  • In the U.S., the average movie ticket price in 2026 is $16.30, with state prices ranging from $12.73 to $25.24. And that’s assuming you’re flying solo or going Dutch.
  • For a movie date night, you’ll fork out $54.08 on average for two tickets and a large popcorn. And that doesn’t include candy, personal pizzas, parking, or a babysitter. (What, are we made of money?)
  • At the expensive end, a New York movie date costs $73.39. At the other end of the scale, Tennesseans pay only $44.73.
  • Savvy savers should know where to find the best deals. At $48.39 for a movie date, Cinemark is the cheapest movie theater chain. Regal is the most expensive at $60.84, with AMC close behind at $55.91.

Average movie ticket price by state

The national average cost for movie tickets is $16.30, ranging from $12.73 per ticket in Arizona to $25.24 per ticket in New York. That means a couple from Arizona can have a movie date for almost exactly what a New Yorker pays for a single ticket.

Movie ticket prices by state

State Average movie ticket cost
Rhode Island n/a
Vermont n/a
Arizona $12.73
Tennessee $12.74
Wisconsin $12.86
Michigan $13.11
Alabama $13.36
Oklahoma $13.74
Mississippi $13.74
Arkansas $14.11
Iowa $14.12
Nebraska $14.17
Montana $14.36
Pennsylvania $14.74
South Carolina $14.74
Utah $14.86
Kansas $14.99
Texas $15.11
Illinois $15.36
Wyoming $15.55
Kentucky $15.74
Louisiana $15.74
South Dakota $15.74
Florida $15.86
Ohio $15.86
Washington $16.24
Alaska $16.37
North Dakota $16.44
Virginia $16.49
Idaho $16.62
Delaware $16.74
North Carolina $16.87
Minnesota $16.98
Connecticut $16.99
Massachusetts $16.99
New Mexico $16.99
Maine $17.19
Indiana $17.49
Maryland $17.49
Missouri $17.49
Colorado $17.74
West Virginia $17.74
Oregon $17.99
New Jersey $18.49
Nevada $18.87
Georgia $19.24
New Hampshire $19.24
Hawaii $20.47
California $20.61
New York $25.24

Average movie date costs by state

The national average for a movie date costs $54.08, but when you see the states side by side, you’ll see dating is a bit more doable in some parts of the country. On the high end, you could pay up to $19.31 more than the national average. At the low end, there’s a $9.35 savings — it’s almost like some states pay you to date!

States where going to the movies is most expensive

A total of 38 states cost above $50 for a movie date night. That’s already pricey, but check this out: Three states (California, Hawaii, and Georgia) surpass the $60 mark — and New York is well above $70. Regionally, you’ll pay more for movie dates on the eastern side of the U.S. But a few notable western exceptions include California, Hawaii, and Nevada.

Rank State Average Date Night Cost
#1 New York $73.39
#2 Hawaii $63.34
#3 California $62.21
#4 Georgia $61.39
#5 New Hampshire $59.67
#6 Nevada $58.92
#7 West Virginia $58.39
#8 New Jersey $57.96
#9 Indiana $57.89
#10 Missouri $57.89

States where going to the movies is least expensive

Which states are the cheapest dates? In 2026, the three states with the most affordable movie date nights are Tennessee, Wisconsin, and Mississippi. There, you’ll pay only $45–$47 to treat your crush.

Rank State Average Date Night Cost
#1 Tennessee $44.73
#2 Wisconsin $46.71
#3 Mississippi $46.73
#4 Michigan $47.21
#5 Iowa $47.48
#6 Alabama $47.71
#7 Arizona $48.18
#8 Oklahoma $48.46
#9 Arkansas $49.21
#10 Montana $49.71
But that means the cheapest movie date in America still costs around $45.00–$50.00 for most people. That’s for one movie. Let’s examine how that compares to “Netflix and chill,” financially.

How theater prices compare to streaming

With subscription streaming services, the movies are almost free because the cost (~$6.99–$32.99 per month) is baked into your monthly budget. So, for the $45–$74 you’d spend on a movie date night, you can watch multiple films (choosing from a hefty library), pop your own popcorn, and maybe even order dinner (and nobody has to ask, “Your place or mine?”). Granted, streaming older movies doesn’t exactly compare to seeing new theatrical releases. A closer comparison is early-access video-on-demand (VOD) rentals. These newer releases rent for $19.99, which is 21%–60% cheaper than movie theater tickets (depending on your region). That’ll leave you with $25–$54 for popcorn, dinner, etc. With streaming and digital rentals, you’ll have to wait for the latest releases, but you’ll pay less than for movie tickets and snacks in most cases.

It’s not all about money; it’s about choice and freedom

Saving money isn’t the only perk when swapping theater dates for home streaming sessions. Streaming means you have more movie choices, you get to stay home, and you might’ve already paid (much less) for snacks. You also don’t have to pay a babysitter. And if nature calls, Ace Ventura-style, you can pause the movie to answer and miss nothing. Streaming works around your life and schedule in ways that theaters simply can’t — plus, it’s less expensive. And it’s worth mentioning, many services like Apple TV, Hulu, Netflix, and Prime Video have their own exclusive movies and shows, too Going to the movies can be an enchanting experience — and the only way to see the weekend drop of the latest blockbusters — but if we’re comparing costs, conveniences, and comfort: Streaming wins every time.

Additional insights

We compared the average cost of a date night between theater chains to see if it pays to be brand loyal — or if we should pick a new favorite theater. It turns out Cinemark is the cheapest chain at $48.39 for two tickets, two drinks, and a large popcorn. That’s $7.50–$12.45 less than AMC ($55.91) and Regal ($60.84).

Tips for saving money on movies

  • Join loyalty programs: Most major theater chains have monthly reward clubs you can join to save on fees, snacks, and ticket prices.
  • Find local discount theaters: If price influences your movie-watching habits, some towns have budget theaters with significantly cheaper tickets and concessions.
  • Look for discount Tuesdays: Cinemark offers discounted tickets ($5–$6) on Tuesdays, but showtimes can sell out quickly. Plan ahead.
  • See a matinee movie: Going during the day means lower prices and fewer crowds.
  • Sneak in snacks: In our Pollfish survey, 71% of respondents said they sneak food and drinks into theaters because theater snacks cost too much.

Pro tip: You can fit a big ol’ burrito in the sleeve of your jacket. Just be cool, and nobody will know.

Methodology

To evaluate the current economic state of the cinema experience, CableTV.com conducted a nationwide survey of 1,000 movie fans. Additionally, ticket pricing data was collected from 48 states to determine the state-by-state minimum, maximum, and average costs for tickets, concessions, and date night packages.
